title: APSARA Authority Hurries up to Repair the Tonle Om Gate Sculptures
highlights:
APSARA National Authority's technical team has been working on reparation project to fix the damaged parts of the sculptures at the Southern Gate of Angkor Thom. Photo by Phoak Chea By:
* Isa Rohany
* ##### **July 28, 2024,**4:46 PM
**
00:00/00:00
**** **SIEM REAP — The APSARA National Authority’s technical team has been working as fast as possible to fix the damaged parts of the sculptures at the Southern Gate of Angkor Thom following a fallen tree incident.** Meng Sovanlylin, architect with the Apsara Authority—the Cambodian government agency managing the Angkor  ...    On July 23, an old teak tree fell due to strong winds on the western side of the Churning of the Ocean of Milk sculpture, causing the death of one person and injuries to three people. According to Sovanlylin, nine statues were also damaged by the tree. These included six statues of asuras, or  ... title: APSARA National Authority Leads Restoration of Angkor Wat’s Second Terrace Causeway - The Better Cambodia
highlights: APSARA National Authority is making significant strides in restoring the causeway at the second terrace of the iconic Angkor Wat Temple. The project, which began in early November 2024, aims to address the extensive deterioration of this historic structure. In a news release this morning, the authority highlighted the progress made by the restoration team in tackling the damage to the centuries-old causeway. According to Mr. Khem Mony, head of the restoration site, the causeway has suffered severe wear and tear over time. Historical photographs from the French colonial era show that the structure  ...    insufficient, with many components—including pillars, floors, and the Naga balustrade—now cracked, broken, or missing  ...    This comprehensive restoration effort reflects the APSARA National Authority’s commitment to preserving Cambodia’s cultural heritage and ensuring that the grandeur of Angkor Wat endures for generations to come. Source: Ministry of Information

title: Broken statue angers temple management | The Phnom Penh Post
highlights: Authority for the Protection and Management of Angkor and the Region of Siem Reap (APSARA) is cooperating with the police in their efforts to arrest a New Zealand national who allegedly knocked over a 26-year-old Buddha statue at Bayon temple in Siem Reap province, according to an official statement from the organisation. Apsara, which manages Angkor Wat and the surrounding ruins, said in the statement that a tuk-tuk driver filed a complaint with heritage police on Thursday after his customer, the suspect, did not return to the vehicle to pay him.  ...    “We are sorry that our statue is damaged. title: Restoration efforts underway at Angkor Thom | The Phnom Penh Post
highlights: APSARA National Authority (ANA) team member recovers the stone head of a damaged sculpture from the baray (moat) at  ...    Restoration efforts are currently underway at the South Gate of Angkor Thom after a recent storm caused significant damage to several ancient sculptures. Kim Sothin, deputy director-general of the APSARA National Authority (ANA), reported that a massive tree fell during the storm, damaging five Asura and three deity sculptures. "An Asura's head was thrown about 10 metres away, and several fragments fell into the water," he stated.  ...
